title: corydalis speciosa var. ramosissima
intro: Corydalis speciosa var. ramosissima, also known as the Korean Corydalis or Han-Byeon-Hwa in traditional Chinese medicine, is a flowering plant species native to East Asia. It is a perennial herb that belongs to the poppy family, Papaveraceae, and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating pain and inflammation. The plant contains compounds such as corydoline and corydaline, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and anticonvulsant properties, making it beneficial for relieving pain, reducing inflammation, and improving mood. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also dried or used in capsule form to harness its medicinal properties.
